name: binary
summary: Produces a flat binary output with no file headers.

name: MZ
summary: Selects the classic DOS MZ (.exe) executable format.

name: PE
summary: Selects the 32-bit Windows Portable Executable format.

name: PE64
summary: Selects the 64-bit Windows Portable Executable format.

name: COFF
summary: Selects the COFF object format (linkable object file for Windows/Unix).

name: MS
summary: Followed by "COFF": selects Microsoft's variant of 32-bit COFF (as produced by MASM/MSVC tools).

name: MS64
summary: Followed by "COFF": selects Microsoft's variant of 64-bit COFF (as produced by MASM/MSVC tools).

name: MachO
summary: Selects the 32-bit Mach-O object/executable format (macOS).

name: MachO64
summary: Selects the 64-bit Mach-O object/executable format (macOS).

name: ELF
summary: Selects the 32-bit ELF format (Linux/Unix). Followed by "executable" or "dynamic" to produce a runnable file instead of a relocatable object.

name: ELF64
summary: Selects the 64-bit ELF format (Linux/Unix). Followed by "executable" or "dynamic" to produce a runnable file instead of a relocatable object.

name: ELFx32
summary: Selects the ELF x32 ABI: x86-64 instructions with 32-bit pointers.

name: executable
summary: Two distinct meanings depending on where it appears. Right after "format": for ELF/ELF64/ELFx32, produces a standalone executable (ET_EXEC) instead of a relocatable object — an optional trailing identifier/number sets the OSABI field, e.g. "format ELF64 executable 3" sets OSABI to 3 (Linux/GNU); 0 = System V, 9 = FreeBSD, 12 = OpenBSD; for MachO/MachO64, produces a runnable image instead of an object file; not a keyword for PE/MZ/COFF, which are executables by default (PE becomes a library only via "DLL"). In a "segment"/"section" attribute list (e.g. "segment readable executable"): marks that segment's pages as executable (ELF PF_X / PE section characteristic) — this is the far more common everyday use.

name: dynamic
summary: For ELF/ELF64 (not ELFx32, which doesn't accept it): produces a position-independent/shared-object executable (ET_DYN), e.g. for PIE binaries or .so libraries.

name: console
summary: PE subsystem: console application (IMAGE_SUBSYSTEM_WINDOWS_CUI).

name: GUI
summary: PE subsystem: windowed/graphical application (IMAGE_SUBSYSTEM_WINDOWS_GUI).

name: native
summary: PE subsystem: native/kernel-mode driver (IMAGE_SUBSYSTEM_NATIVE).

name: DLL
summary: Marks the PE output as a dynamic-link library rather than an executable.

name: WDM
summary: Marks the PE output as a Windows Driver Model (WDM) driver.

name: efi
summary: PE subsystem: UEFI application (IMAGE_SUBSYSTEM_EFI_APPLICATION).

name: EFIboot
summary: PE subsystem: UEFI boot service driver.

name: EFIruntime
summary: PE subsystem: UEFI runtime driver.

name: large
summary: Sets the PE "large address aware" flag (IMAGE_FILE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE).

name: NX
summary: Sets the PE "NX compatible" flag, opting into DEP/data execution prevention.

name: on
summary: Introduces the DOS stub program for a PE image, e.g. "format PE on 'stub.exe'".

name: readable
summary: Segment/section attribute: pages are readable (ELF PF_R / PE section characteristic).

name: writeable
summary: Segment/section attribute: pages are writable (ELF PF_W / PE section characteristic).

name: discardable
summary: PE section attribute: the section may be discarded from memory after loading.

name: shareable
summary: PE section attribute: the section's pages are shared between all instances of the process.

name: interpreter
summary: ELF segment attribute: marks the PT_INTERP segment holding the dynamic linker's path.

name: note
summary: ELF segment attribute: marks a PT_NOTE segment.

name: gnustack
summary: ELF segment attribute: marks the PT_GNU_STACK segment, which controls whether the stack is executable.

name: gnuehframe
summary: ELF segment attribute: marks the PT_GNU_EH_FRAME segment (exception-handling frame info).

name: gnurelro
summary: ELF segment attribute: marks the PT_GNU_RELRO segment (made read-only after relocations are applied).
